batches.name: Cluster A Wave 1 - Developer Apps
batches.rationale: All 5 apps in Cluster A are independent - separate codebases, no shared dependencies. Can build in parallel.

tasks.title: Build IncidentBrain - AI Incident Root Cause Analysis
tasks.prompt:
Build IncidentBrain, an AI-powered incident root cause analysis platform.

CONTEXT:
- This is part of a 91-app micro-SaaS ecosystem on shared cc-starter foundation
- Tech stack: Spring Boot 3.3.5 + cc-starter (provides tenancy, auth, RBAC, audit, search, AI, webhooks), PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector, Redis 7, Keycloak, LiteLLM
- Foundation repo: https://github.com/Shashank2577/micro-saas (already has Nexus Hub orchestrator)
- Use scaffold-app.sh pattern from tools/ to bootstrap app structure
- Must integrate with Nexus Hub for app registration and event bus

REQUIREMENTS from spec:
1. Incident ingestion: Webhook endpoints for incident creation (Datadog/PagerDuty format)
2. Context aggregation: Fetch correlated logs, metrics, traces, git history
3. AI Analysis: Use Claude Sonnet 4.6 via LiteLLM for root cause hypothesis
4. Similarity search: Vector search over historical incidents (pgvector)
5. Postmorter generator: Auto-generate markdown postmortem draft

MVP Scope:
- Manual incident creation form
- Datadog integration for logs/metrics (API fetch)
- GitHub integration for recent commits
- AI root cause analysis (Claude via LiteLLM)
- Simple incident timeline UI

DATA MODEL:
- Incident entity: id, title, severity, status, rootCauseHypothesis, confidenceScore, timelineEvents[], similarIncidents[], postmortemDraft
- TimelineEvent: timestamp, source, type, summary, metadata
- RootCauseCandidate: description, confidence, evidence[], aiReasoning

INTEGRATION MANIFEST:
- emits: incident.opened, incident.root-cause-identified, incident.resolved
- consumes: deploy.completed, error-rate-spike, alert-fired
- capabilities: analyze-incident, find-similar, generate-postmortem

IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:
1. Use scaffold-app.sh to bootstrap: ./tools/scaffold-app.sh incidentbrain "IncidentBrain" 8091
2. Create entities in src/main/java/com/microsaas/incidentbrain/
3. Create REST controllers with OpenAPI docs
4. Implement AI analysis service using LiteLLM (Claude Sonnet 4.6)
5. Create integration-manifest.json
6. Build simple Next.js frontend with incident list/timeline
7. Test with Nexus Hub registration

Keep code clean, follow Spring Boot best practices, use cc-starter modules for tenancy/auth/audit.

tasks.title: Build DependencyRadar - Open Source Dependency Monitoring
tasks.prompt:
Build DependencyRadar, an open source dependency monitoring platform with upgrade intelligence.

CONTEXT:
- Part of 91-app micro-SaaS ecosystem on shared cc-starter foundation
- Tech stack: Spring Boot 3.3.5 + cc-starter, PostgreSQL, Redis, Keycloak, LiteLLM
- Foundation repo: https://github.com/Shashank2577/micro-saas
- Must integrate with Nexus Hub for event bus

REQUIREMENTS from spec:
1. Repo scanning: GitHub webhook on push to parse dependency files (pom.xml, package.json)
2. Vulnerability feed: Cross-reference with NVD and GitHub Security Advisories
3. Dependency graph: Build transitive dependency tree
4. Impact analyzer: For upgrade candidate, run test suite in sandbox
5. Auto-PR: Create pull request with upgrade if tests pass

MVP Scope:
- GitHub webhook on push
- Maven + npm dependency parsing
- NVD vulnerability lookup
- Manual 'test upgrade' button (runs tests in background)
- Dependency health dashboard

DATA MODEL:
- Dependency: repoId, name, currentVersion, latestVersion, vulnerabilities[], outdated, deprecated
- Vulnerability: cveId, severity, description, affectedVersions, patchedVersions
- UpgradeCandidate: dependencyId, newVersion, testResults[], prUrl

INTEGRATION MANIFEST:
- emits: vulnerability-detected, dependency-outdated, upgrade-available
- consumes: code.pushed, pr.merged
- capabilities: scan-dependencies, check-upgrade, create-upgrade-pr

IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:
1. Scaffold: ./tools/scaffold-app.sh dependencyradar "DependencyRadar" 8092
2. Create GitHub webhook controller for push events
3. Parse pom.xml/package.json with Maven/ npm APIs
4. Implement NVD API client for vulnerability lookup
5. Create dependency health dashboard UI
6. Manual test upgrade feature (background job with tests)
7. Register with Nexus Hub

tasks.title: Build DeploySignal - Deployment Analytics and Risk Assessment
tasks.prompt:
Build DeploySignal, a deployment analytics platform with risk assessment.

CONTEXT:
- Part of 91-app micro-SaaS ecosystem on cc-starter foundation
- Tech stack: Spring Boot 3.3.5 + cc-starter, TimescaleDB (or Postgres + Timescale extension), Redis, Keycloak
- Foundation repo: https://github.com/Shashank2577/micro-saas

REQUIREMENTS from spec:
1. Deploy ingestion: Webhook from CI/CD (GitHub Actions, Vercel, CircleCI)
2. Risk scoring: Algorithm weighting commit count, test failures, deployer tenure, time/day
3. DORA dashboard: Deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, time to restore
4. Anomaly detection: Spike in error rate after deploy

MVP Scope:
- GitHub Actions webhook ingestion
- Basic risk scoring (files changed count, test pass/fail)
- Deploy history table
- Simple DORA metrics (deploy freq, change fail rate)

DATA MODEL:
- Deployment: id, sha, branch, status, startedAt, completedAt, riskScore, filesChanged[], testResults, rollback
- RiskFactor: type, weight, description

INTEGRATION MANIFEST:
- emits: deploy.started, deploy.completed, deploy.failed, deploy.rolled-back
- consumes: incident.opened, error-rate-spike, test.failed
- capabilities: calculate-risk, track-dora

IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:
1. Scaffold: ./tools/scaffold-app.sh deploysignal "DeploySignal" 8093
2. Create GitHub Actions webhook controller
3. Implement risk scoring algorithm
4. Create DORA metrics calculation queries
5. Build deploy calendar UI
6. Register with Nexus Hub

tasks.title: Build APIEvolver - API Contract Management with Backward Compatibility
tasks.prompt:
Build APIEvolver, an API contract management platform with backward compatibility checking.

CONTEXT:
- Part of 91-app micro-SaaS ecosystem on cc-starter foundation
- Tech stack: Spring Boot 3.3.5 + cc-starter, PostgreSQL, Redis, Keycloak
- Foundation repo: https://github.com/Shashank2577/micro-saas

REQUIREMENTS from spec:
1. Spec registry: Upload OpenAPI/GraphQL specs for each service version
2. Spec diff: Compare v1 vs v2, list changed endpoints
3. Breaking change detector: Rules engine for breaking changes
4. Dependency graph: Service A depends on Service B's API
5. Change notification: Notify consumers on API changes

MVP Scope:
- Manual OpenAPI spec upload (JSON file)
- Spec diff between two versions
- Manual breaking change rules (simple regex/list)
- Manual consumer registration

DATA MODEL:
- ApiSpec: serviceId, version, specType, specUrl, contentHash, uploadedAt
- ApiChange: specId, oldVersion, newVersion, changes[], breakingChanges[]
- ApiDependency: consumerServiceId, providerServiceId, usedEndpoints[]

INTEGRATION MANIFEST:
- emits: api-spec-uploaded, breaking-change-detected, api-deprecated
- consumes: service.registered, api-spec-scanned
- capabilities: diff-specs, detect-breaking, notify-consumers

IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:
1. Scaffold: ./tools/scaffold-app.sh apievolver "APIEvolver" 8094
2. Create OpenAPI spec upload endpoint
3. Implement spec diff algorithm (swagger-parser library)
4. Build breaking change detector rules engine
5. Create spec visualization UI (side-by-side diff)
6. Register with Nexus Hub

tasks.title: Build SecurityPulse - Code Security Scanner with Policy Enforcement
tasks.prompt:
Build SecurityPulse, a unified code security scanner with policy enforcement.

CONTEXT:
- Part of 91-app micro-SaaS ecosystem on cc-starter foundation
- Tech stack: Spring Boot 3.3.5 + cc-starter, PostgreSQL, Redis, Keycloak, LiteLLM
- Foundation repo: https://github.com/Shashank2577/micro-saas

REQUIREMENTS from spec:
1. PR scanning: GitHub Actions app that scans on every PR
2. Tool orchestration: Run Semgrep (SAST), TruffleHog (secrets), custom patterns
3. Policy engine: Rule-based (block PR on secret, warn on high SAST)
4. AI triage: Claude Sonnet 4.6 groups findings, filters FPs, suggests fixes
5. Issue tracker: Track findings over time

MVP Scope:
- Manual scan (paste branch name/SHA)
- Semgrep + TruffleHog integration
- Basic policy engine (block on secrets, warn on high severity)
- Findings table

DATA MODEL:
- SecurityScan: prId, sha, branch, status, findings[], policyDecision
- Finding: tool, severity, type, filePath, line, description, aiSuggestion
- Policy: name, rules[], action

INTEGRATION MANIFEST:
- emits: security-scan-completed, vulnerability-found, policy-violation
- consumes: pr.opened, pr.merged
- capabilities: scan-pr, enforce-policy, triage-findings

IMPLEMENTATION STEPS:
1. Scaffold: ./tools/scaffold-app.sh securitypulse "SecurityPulse" 8095
2. Integrate Semgrep CLI and TruffleHog CLI
3. Create manual scan endpoint (branch + SHA)
4. Implement policy engine with rule evaluation
5. Build security dashboard UI
6. Register with Nexus Hub

Keep it simple for MVP, no GitHub Actions app yet (manual scan trigger).
